name: Benthos / Redpanda Connect Stream Processor
what_it_does: Open-source stream processor with REST management API — enabling engineers to build declarative data pipelines using YAML configuration to connect inputs (Kafka, HTTP, S3, databases) through processors (transformations, enrichments, filtering) to outputs (databases, queues, HTTP, cloud storage), with 200+ connectors, a functional expression language (Bloblang), and embedded HTTP API for management and injection.
use_cases: Building Kafka-to-database ETL pipelines via Benthos YAML for data engineering workflows
Enriching event streams via Benthos processors for real-time data transformation
Routing messages between queues via Benthos for fan-out and content-based routing
Running HTTP-to-message-queue bridge via Benthos for API-to-stream ingestion
Replacing custom Go ETL code with declarative Benthos pipelines for operational simplicity
not_for: Complex stateful stream processing with joins and windows (use Apache Flink or Spark Streaming)
Teams preferring code-based pipelines over YAML declarative config (use Go/Python streaming libraries)
Enterprise with Oracle/TIBCO EAI dependencies needing commercial support (use MuleSoft)
best_when: A data engineering team needs to connect 200+ data sources/sinks with declarative YAML pipelines — where Benthos/Redpanda Connect handles routing, transformation, and enrichment without writing custom streaming code.
avoid_when: You need stateful stream processing with complex windowing/joins, prefer code over YAML, or need commercial enterprise integration platform support.

auth.notes: No built-in authentication for management API by default. HTTP management API (POST /post for message injection, GET /ready, /ping, /stats). Credentials configured per-connector (Kafka, S3, etc. have their own auth). Runs as sidecar or standalone process — no central auth. Basic auth can be added via HTTP server config. Redpanda Cloud: connects uses Redpanda's auth. Self-hosted: user-managed security.

pricing.paid_tiers: Redpanda Cloud includes managed Connect; Enterprise support via Redpanda
pricing.notes: Created by Ashley Jeffs (2016). Originally called Benthos. Acquired by Redpanda 2023, rebranded as Redpanda Connect. Apache 2.0 license (maintained). GitHub: 8,500+ stars as benthos. 200+ connectors: AWS (S3, SQS, DynamoDB, Kinesis), GCP (Pub/Sub, BigQuery), Azure (Storage, Event Hubs), Kafka, RabbitMQ, HTTP, databases, Redis, etc. Bloblang: custom expression language for message transformation. YAML-first declarative config. No JVM — lightweight Go binary. Embedded HTTP API for management. Redpanda Cloud integration (managed deployment). Previously Jeffail/benthos on GitHub, now redpanda-data/connect.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — bounded by hardware and connector limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST management API (not messaging API). YAML declarative configuration. 200+ connectors. No auth by default on management API. Acquired by Redpanda 2023 (rebranded Redpanda Connect). Bloblang for transformations. Go binary — no JVM dependency. Excellent documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline idempotency depends on input/output connector idempotency. At-least-once delivery model. Processors can implement deduplication. Bloblang supports stateless transformation (idempotent by nature).
known_agent_gotchas: Rebranded from Benthos to Redpanda Connect 2023 — documentation at both www.benthos.dev and docs.redpanda.com
GitHub repo moved from Jeffail/benthos to redpanda-data/connect
Management API at HTTP port 4195 by default — no auth, restrict network access
Bloblang is a custom expression language — not JavaScript/JQ/Python; must learn separately
YAML-declarative — not code; complex logic in Bloblang can be harder to test/debug
200+ connectors but connector-specific auth must be configured per-input/output

security_score_components.security_notes: No built-in auth on management API — restrict by network. Apache 2.0 open source — auditable Go code. User-controlled data residency. Each connector uses its own auth (Kafka, S3, etc.). Bloblang processors run in sandboxed Go — no arbitrary code execution. Secret values can reference environment variables in YAML. TLS configurable per-connector.
